programmes.show_name: Romesh Ranganathan: For the Love of Hip Hop
programmes.show_description: Romesh invites AJ Tracey to select his 10 Minute Mixtape. They discuss AJ's musical background and his latest album, Don't Die Before You're Dead.
Tony Blackburn returns with a joyous selection of Soul and celebrates 66 years since the official launch of Motown Records - including classics from Stevie Wonder and The Supremes.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1989.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1995.
Del Amitri performing live at Wolverhampton Civic Hall in 1998.  Featuring Always The Last To Know, Driving With The Brakes On, Move Away Jimmy Blue and Kiss This Thing Goodbye.
Time to Spring Clean? It'll fly by with Angela Griffin motivating you with songs to spruce up and declutter. Featuring Diana Ross, TLC and Taylor Swift and more.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 60s with Tony Blackburn
programmes.show_description: Tony brings you two hours of music from the best decade ever, with hits from Barbara Lewis, Roy Orbison and Andy Williams. This week, the top three comes from 1962.
Comedian & podcaster James Acaster is in the studio. Plus, a wedding call and The Mystery Voice. To listen on most smart speakers just say 'Ask BBC Sounds to play Dermot O'Leary'.
Jamie Laing joins Romesh live in the studio to chat about his new Disney+ series, Raising Chelsea.
Spend Saturday lunchtime with Emma. Whether you're out and about or chilling at home, Emma is standing-by to speak to you and hit shuffle on all your favourite music.
Rylan's joined by pop icon Peter Andre to talk about his new music and sings the icon hit Mysterious Girl in KaRYoke. Plus, will there be a new champion in Rylan's film & TV quiz?
The teatime mischief includes more of your dad gags and incongruous sightings of the famous, as Shaun plays a joyful mix of music and catches up on messages from the murmuration.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 80s with Gary Davies
programmes.show_description: Soul legend Jocelyn Brown joins Gary in the second hour, looking back at our favourite decade.

programmes.show_name: Sounds of the 90s with Fearne Cotton
programmes.show_description: Gary Davies sticks around to decide which is the ultimate decade... 80s or 90s?! He and Fearne are also sharing Top of the Pops hosting memories and looking back at Gary's 90s.
A playlist celebrating Bob's 80th birthday.

programmes.show_name: Dance Sounds of the 90s with Vernon Kay
programmes.show_description: Vernon mixes an hour of euphoric, uplifting trance classics. Pumping bass lines and amazing melodies from Planet Perfecto, Liquid Child, Paul van Dyk, Chicane and William Orbit.
Tony rolls out fabulous soul anthems to get you moving and dancing, with great tracks from Sister Sledge, George Benson, Michael Jackson, Jennifer Holiday and more!
Trouble Sleeping? Angela Griffin provides a playlist of songs for you to nod off to. Featuring Bill Withers, David Bowie and Arlo Parks and more.
Al Stewart performing live at Cambridge Folk Festival in 1989. Featuring Time Passages, Nostradamus, On The Border, Roads To Moscow and Year Of The Cat.
Actor Eddie Marsan OBE joins Mark Goodier for an in-depth conversation about his life, choosing ten songs which have soundtracked that time so far.
The Reverend Kate Bottley and Jason Mohammad spread hope and joy, with uplifting stories about life, love and modern spirituality, alongside a great soundtrack for Sunday morning.
Your stories, dedications and classic love songs with Michael... easy like Sunday morning.
Paddy's live from Salford every Sunday and he's got another BIG question for you. Plus, The Weekest Link quiz Paddy's Timewarp and your Sunday Dinners.
Dame Elaine's got show tunes, movie songs, stage news and your Break A Leg messages, plus, we'll be hearing from a selection of 2026 Olivier Award nominees.

programmes.show_name: Bob Harris At 80: A Life In Music
programmes.show_description: Radio 2 raise a very stylish, very rock 'n' roll glass to broadcasting legend, Bob Harris, at 80. Zoe Ball leads the celebration with Bob and delves into his milestones and music.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1979.
Mark Goodier counts down the chart from this day in 1986.
Tony Blackburn spins his personal pick of the hits from across the decades. To listen on most smart speakers just 'ask BBC Sounds to play Tony Blackburn's Golden Hour'.
Join Jo Whiley for the best seats in the house as we celebrate The Olivier Awards, which return to the magnificent Royal Albert Hall to mark their 50th anniversary.
Take time out with Radio 2 Unwinds, featuring Amii Stewart, Commodores and Bill Withers.

programmes.show_name: Radio 2 Unwinds Mix with Angela Griffin
programmes.show_description: The Musical Unwinds Mix with Angela Griffin, featuring relaxing music from the stage and screen. Expect iconic tracks from the likes of Chess, Annie, Xanadu and Evita.
